resources.name: Centro CILS, Università per Stranieri di Siena
resources.why: The state university that designs and awards CILS, one of the four CLIQ certifications the Ministero dell'Interno accepts for citizenship: exam dates, official formats, sample papers and the network of exam centres, the finish line this app's mock exams mirror.

resources.name: CVCL, Università per Stranieri di Perugia (esami CELI)
resources.why: The second CLIQ body, awarding CELI. Useful for two reasons: its centres may be closer to you than Siena's, and its pass rule is different, summing the written parts instead of demanding sufficiency in each, so compare before you book.

resources.name: PLIDA, Società Dante Alighieri
resources.why: The third accepted certification, run through more than 300 centres in over 60 countries, so it is often the only CLIQ exam you can sit without travelling to Italy. Calendars, regulations and a centre finder are all on the site.

resources.name: Vocabolario Treccani
resources.why: The reference dictionary of standard Italian, free online from the Istituto della Enciclopedia Italiana, with the encyclopedia and the synonym dictionary alongside it. Use it to check that a word is standard Italian and not regional.

resources.name: Easy Italian
resources.why: Street interviews with real Italians, every video subtitled in Italian and English at once, plus a slower series for beginners. This is how you bridge from the app's clean audio to the speed and messiness of real speech.

resources.name: OnlineItalianClub
resources.why: Thousands of free pages of grammar drills, listening exercises, graded dialogues and verb tables sorted by CEFR level, with no signup: the cheapest way to get extra written practice at exactly the level you are working through here.

resources.name: Conversation partner
resources.why: Produzione orale is a scored CILS ability that no app can replace: a tutor, a language exchange or an Italian friend turns these dialogues into speech. Ask for standard Italian, since a partner who speaks mostly in dialect will not prepare you for the exam.

resources.name: Words tab (built-in daily flashcards)
resources.why: The app's own spaced-repetition deck: high-frequency Italian banded from A1 to B1 with example sentences and audio, scheduled into every session. Vocabulary is the one thing that only accumulates with daily contact, so this is the habit that carries the rest.
